People: At 1PM the command of the US Steamer Kearsarge was conveyed to Commander James. S. Thornton, the Colors and Pennant being hoisted, she was placed in commission. The orders from the Navy Department placing Commander Thornton in command of the vessel, was read upon the quarter deck by Lt. Com'dr McCook in presence of all officers attached to this vessel.

Other: 2130: Wind came out suddenly from NE  and continued to blow in terrific squalls from that quarter.

Ice_Log: 0400: Floating ice covering the harbor, mentioned up to 2000hrs.
1800: Large quantities of ice passing by.

Note: 1920: Cape Cod brg South 7mi cc ExS

Other: 2130: Wind came out suddenly from NE  and continued to blow in terrific squalls from that quarter.

People: 0530: Called away the dingey & instead of the proper crew the following named men lowered her & deserted from the ship. Sent a boat & brought back the dingey but did not recover any of the deserters.

Other: 2000: Indications of an Earthquake experienced during the watch. Tremulous motion to ship and agitation in the water. Called !All hands! at 10 secured boats battery as for sea. Started fires at 10:40. Piped down at 11:20.  fan forced ??came in at an interval of half an hour, occasioning a rise of about 20ft in the water which flooded the mole?? causing considerable damage to the shipping. http://www.drgeorgepc.com/Tsunami1868Chile.html
2100: Slight shock of earthquake. Heavy swell.
0400: Several earthquake shocks were experienced during the watch.

People: Noon: Rec'd on board Charles Sorrell (M. at A.) & Aug. Johnson (Qr M) survivors of the wreck of the Store Ship "Fredonia, without the necessary accompanying lists.
Sent John Smith Capt. C. & Geo. W. Broom (1st Boy) deserters from the Powhatan to that vessel, and also, two men Chars. Sorrell (M. at A.) and Aug. Johnson (Qr. M.) survivors of the Freedonia.

Other: 0800: Crfew and cutters engaged in transferring stores from USS Wateree to Powhatan of the next two days.

Noon: Running for the entrance of the Biquerm de pisco ??
